description_long: This house features an exposed wooden truss roof structure with a prominent brick chimney running through the center, suggesting a traditional residential design. The framework reveals a spacious, open-plan interior with visible support beams and rafters, indicating a single-story layout. The roof is a hip style, sloping gently on all sides, and the structure includes a covered porch area on one side. The use of brick for the chimney and wood for the framing is evident, with the absence of exterior wall cladding exposing the skeletal construction. The house appears to be designed for suburban or rural settings, with ample space for windows and doors, though these are not yet installed. The overall form is rectangular with a symmetrical roofline and a central chimney as a focal point.
description: A single-story house with an exposed wooden truss roof and a central brick chimney, featuring a hip roof and open framework. The structure includes a covered porch and is designed in a traditional residential style.
description_view_0: Side view showing the hip roof structure, central brick chimney, and open wooden trusses with a covered porch visible on the right.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the symmetrical roofline, central chimney, and the open framework of the porch area.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the hip roof, central chimney, and exposed wooden beams, with the porch area on the left.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the hip roof, central chimney, and the open truss structure without visible porch.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with steps, central door, and open framework for future installation of entry features
